steps.description: Click the "Search" input field in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Tab" option in the "Platform capability" dropdown menu within the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Language" dropdown in the "Samples" workspace of Microsoft Visual Studio Code to open the language filter menu.
Click the "JS" option in the "Language" dropdown menu within the Samples view of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Create" button next to "Contact Exporter using Graph Toolkit" in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit Samples tab within Visual Studio Code.

Click the blue "Create" button for the "Contact Exporter using Graph Toolkit" sample in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit Samples tab within Visual Studio Code.Add ${{var:app_name:contact-exporter}} in to env.
Click the "Reload this page" button in the Chrome browser toolbar to refresh the Microsoft Teams web app tab.
Type 'https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab' into the Chrome browser's address bar on a new tab displaying the Google homepage.
Press Enter to select the highlighted URL (https://localhost:53000/index.html#/tab) in the Chrome address bar on a new tab page.
Click the "Advanced" button on the Chrome privacy error page at https://localhost:53000, indicated by a bordered button below the warning message.
Click the "Proceed to localhost (unsafe)" link on the Chrome privacy error page to bypass the security warning and continue to the localhost site.
Click the "Microsoft Teams Tab" browser tab at the top left of the Google Chrome window to activate it.
Click the red plus ("+") icon in the top-right corner of the "team-central-dashboardlocal" panel within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Reload" option in the context menu of a Microsoft Teams web app tab within Google Chrome.
Click the "Allow" button in the Microsoft Teams browser permission popup to grant access for connecting to devices on your local network.
Click the blue "Start" button in the center of the Contact Exporter App welcome screen within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Accept" button on the Microsoft permissions request dialog to grant app permissions.
@assertion there's "Current Login Account" exist.
Click the "Search for a name" input field in the Contact Table section of the contact-exporterlocal app within Microsoft Teams (web interface).
Type 'tes' into the search input field above the Contact Table in the contact-exporterlocal app within Microsoft Teams (web interface).
Click on the "test21" contact suggestion in the dropdown list below the search bar within the Contact Table section of the contact-exporterlocal app in the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Type 't' into the search input field above the Contact Table in the contact-exporterlocal web app within Microsoft Teams (browser interface).
Click the "test25" contact button in the Contact Table section of the contact-exporterlocal app running in Microsoft Teams (web version).
Click on the "test25" entry within the contact search field of the "Contact Table" section in the contact-exporterlocal app in Microsoft Teams (web interface).
Enter 'test25' into the Contact Table search input field within the contact-exporterlocal app in Microsoft Teams (web interface).

Click on the user suggestion "test25" (test25@xxbdw.onmicrosoft.com) in the dropdown list of the search field within the "Current Login Account" panel of the contact-exporterlocal app in Microsoft Teams (web interface).
Press the End key while the "Search for a name" input field is focused in the Contact Table section of the contact-exporterlocal Microsoft Teams web application.
Click the "Export Contact Table to Excel" button at the bottom right of the contact-exporterlocal tab in the Microsoft Teams web app interface.
